states.name: Aria
states.title: Petite Primordial
states.description:
Aria, like all other Primordials, was born at the foundation of her world. Gifted with power over death, her duty was to judge mortal souls as they passed to the afterlife. With a touch, she could live each person's greatest triumphs and vilest sins, and with a swing of her scythe, she could snuff out any mortal life she pleased.

Aria could never understand why the living feared her. Unlike other Primordials, she wandered their world as she pleased, desperate to understand those she would one day judge. But all who saw her ran and cursed her name, beseeching their gods to protect them from the specter of death.

Eventually, a Goddess answered their prayers.

states.name: Aria
states.title: Petite Primordial
states.description:
Aria doesn't remember much of her world's first True God. She's lived a long, long time, and shared too many memories to keep them all straight. But she does remember the day Orcanix first arrived. It was the day she realized she, too, might someday die.

The Undead Goddess commanded her hordes to hunt down and destroy the Primordial of Death, the one power capable of opposing her. Aria begged the mortals for shelter, but all turned her away, too terrified of Orcanix's assassins to risk her ire.

Only one man proved brave enough to protect her. A noble lord from the petty kingdom of Sanga, who foresaw the destruction to come.

states.name: Aria
states.title: Petite Primordial
states.description:
The lord and his trusted bodyguard offered Aria their hospitality, letting her do as she liked within their lands. At first, Aria mistrusted them, too used to mortal terror to accept their kindness blindly. But after years of their unflinching protection in the face of the undead, her legendarily prickly shell gradually gave way.

After an encounter with Orcanix's assassins left the lord's family dead, she decided she could no longer endanger them with her presence. She sealed herself inside her Primordial realm, asking her guardians to summon her only at their most desperate. Her imprisonment would ensure Orcanix never created a world without death, and more importantly, it would keep the two of them safe.

She would just have to get used to being alone again.
